creator: jason@aventia.pw
text:
has the git port been changed to specifically not be updatable via portmaster?


====> Compressing man pages (compress-man)
===> Staging rc.d startup script(s)

===>>> Creating a backup package for old version git-2.39.2
Creating package for git-2.39.2
pkg-static: Cannot get an advisory lock on a database, it is locked by another process

===>>> Starting check for runtime dependencies
===>>> Gathering dependency list for devel/git@default from ports
===>>> Dependency check complete for devel/git@default

===>>> All >> git-2.39.2 (6/27)

===>  Installing for git-2.40.1
===>  Checking if git is already installed
===>   An older version of git is already installed (git-2.39.2)
      You may wish to ``make deinstall'' and install this port again
      by ``make reinstall'' to upgrade it properly.
      If you really wish to overwrite the old port of git
      without deleting it first, set the variable "FORCE_PKG_REGISTER"
      in your environment or the "make install" command line.
*** Error code 1

Stop.
make[1]: stopped in /usr/ports/devel/git
*** Error code 1

Stop.
make: stopped in /usr/ports/devel/git

===>>> A backup package for git-2.39.2 should
       be located in /usr/ports/packages/portmaster-backup

===>>> Installation of git-2.40.1 (devel/git@default) failed
===>>> Aborting update

===>>> Update for devel/git@default failed
===>>> Aborting update
